characters.name: IgglePiggle.
characters.description: This article is astub. You can help In The Night Garden Wiki byexpanding it. Reason: Needs more information. IgglePiggle, sometimes spelt asIgglepiggle, is one of 6 main characters inIn the Night Garden.... He is portrayed byNick Kellington. IgglePiggleNameIgglePiggleGenderMalePlayed byNick KellingtonFirst appearanceMakka Pakka Washes Faces IgglePiggle is one of the 6 main characters of In the Night Garden..., He is stated to be a teddy bear, yet he bares little resemblance to one. As one of the more well known residents ofthe Night Garden, IgglePiggle is respected by his friends who are happy to join him in whatever he might be doing. In particular IgglePiggle is best friends withUpsy Daisy.

characters.name: Pontipines
characters.description: The PontipinesNameThe PontipinesGenderMale (4 children, Mr Pontipine)Female (4 children, Mrs Pontipine)First appearanceMakka Pakka Washes Faces Female (4 children, Mrs Pontipine) The Pontipines are a family of ten tiny people (a mother and a father plus four boys and four girls who are eight tiny children), similar to peg dolls. They live in a semidetached house at the foot of a tree. They are frequent passengers of theNinky Nonkand have their own carriage, which is shared withthe Wottingers. Whilst the parents always have good feasts, the children are fedrunny grobbles.

characters.name: The Haahoos
characters.description: The HaahoosNameThe HaahoosGenderNonePlayed by10ft inflatablesFirst appearanceThe Tombliboos' Waving Game "The Haahoos. Haahoooooo!" -Derek Jacobi The Haahoos are a group of recurring characters on the show. They are a group of sentient inflatables that often bounce around deeper forested areas of the garden, usually around theTombliboo Bush. They can usually be seen in groups of 2 and/or 3. Their calm nature and the fact they seem to be the least annoying and repetitive characters on the show make them a fan-favourite.

characters.name: The Pinky Ponk
characters.description: The Pinky PonkNameThe Pinky PonkPlayed byA remote controlled zeppelinFirst appearanceThe Tombliboos' Waving Game The Pinky Ponkis one of a few recurring characters that appear inIn The Night Garden.... It is a colourful airship that can control itself. (likethe Ninky Nonk.) The Pinky Ponk is coloured a bright turquoise and is decorated with an abundance of pink, orange and yellow speckles on each side. The "cockpit" contains multiple small tables that house thepinky ponk juicefor each character to drink on their journey and are surrounded by small stones acting as stools. at the front is a huge window for them to look out of and enjoy the spectacular views of the Night Garden. On the top is a tiny deck especially forthe Pontipines, who access the Pinky Ponk through a tiny door and go up a lift to enter that deck. The bigger characters enter through a similarly sized door so they could get in too. On the outside are 4 rows of orange-speckled wings and tiny purple propellers to make the body afloat. The back of the vehicle houses a ginormous pink propeller in front of a back deck where the passengers can get some fresh air. At the front is a transparent magenta light that is triggered when the Pinky Ponk is about to take off, land, or, well, ponk.

characters.name: The Zonky Flonk
characters.description: The Zonky FlonkNameThe Zonky FlonkFirst appearanceMakka Pakka (ZinkyZonk episode) The Zonky Flonkis a minor character inIn the Night Garden... ZinkyZonk Specials. It is an open-roofed vehicle that transports other characters to the ZonkyZoney when they need a zinky zonk.

characters.name: Tittifers
characters.description: This article is astub. You can help In The Night Garden Wiki byexpanding it. Reason: No reason given TheTittifersare a bunch of CGI enhanced tropical birds with their own unique songs. There are four small blue tittifers (rufous-crowned laughingthrushes), three larger pink ones (hoopoes), two big green ones (white-cheeked turacos) and one Channel-billed toucan. They usually sing between segments, and when they sing together, near the end, it is time for the inhabitants, including the Haahoos and the vehicles to go to sleep. They were first featured in the episode "Makka Pakka Washes Faces", along with Igglepiggle, Upsy Daisy, Makka Pakka, the Tombliboos, the Pontipines, the Haahoos, the Ninky Nonk, and the Pinky Ponk. https://inthenightgarden.fandom.com/wiki/File:The_Tittifers.mp4

characters.name: Tombliboo Unn
characters.description: UnnNameUnnGenderMalePlayed byAndy WarehamFirst appearanceMakka Pakka Washes FacesUnn(Played by Andy Wareham) is 1 of the 3 majorTombliboocharacters fromIn the Night Garden... Tombliboo Unn is part of theTombliboos, a trio of characters appearing in the show. He lives in theTombliboo BushwithOooandEee, who are also Tombliboos. Like the others, he often walks outside in the garden with Ooo and Eee, playing along the way, takes a trip on thePinky-PonkorNinky-Nonk, sometimes encountering theHaahoosalong the way. Unn has a light skin tone, plain black eyes and 5 red balls on his head representing his hair. He wears a red and green striped top (possibly part of his body as a whole) and red trousers with green spots on each side. Tombliboo Unn is part of theTombliboos, a trio of characters appearing in the show. He lives in theTombliboo BushwithOooandEee, who are also Tombliboos. Like the others, he often walks outside in the garden with Ooo and Eee, playing along the way, takes a trip on thePinky-PonkorNinky-Nonk, sometimes encountering theHaahoosalong the way.

characters.name: Tombliboos
characters.description: The Tombliboosare a trio of characters dressed in costumes with stripes and spots: red and green (Unn), brown and pink (Ooo), and pink and yellow (Eee). Their names reflect phonetically how a young child might count to three, and the Tombliboos always appear in this order by saying their names. They live among the branches of a bush, on a series of platforms connected by stairs and chutes. The Tombliboos enjoy playing the drums and piano, although not with any particular musical prowess, and they also play with large blocks. Their trousers (which fall down unexpectedly) are often seen hanging on the washing line outside their home, and they usually take them down from the line and put them on before going on an adventure. They are sometimes seen brushing their teeth before bed. Tombliboos enjoy kissing each other whilst cuddling and reciting their names. They also have a song about staying together, which consists of them singing "Tombliboo, Tombliboo, Unn, Ooo, Eee!" over and over again. Tombliboo Eee is female and is shorter and smaller than Ooo and Unn. The Tombliboos, along with Iggle Piggle, Upsy Daisy and Makka Pakka were first featured in the episode "Makka Pakka Washes Faces". Playing Outside Playing With Bricks

characters.name: Upsy Daisy
characters.description: This article is astub. You can help In The Night Garden Wiki byexpanding it. Reason: No reason given Upsy Daisyis one of the six main characters of theIn the Night Garden...series. In the original series she is portrayed byRebecca Hyland. She is similar to a ragdoll, with a brown complexion, a chime in her tummy, and brightly coloured clothes. She likes skipping through the Garden and blowing kisses to the other characters or the audience. She often hugs and kisses other characters, particularly Igglepiggle. Her catchphrases are "Upsy Daisy," and the more emphatic "Daisy Doo!", and she also says "Pip pip onk onk!", her way of saying goodbye. Her hair stands on end when excited or surprised (whenever she says “Oooh!” or “Oooh, Upsy Daisy!”), and her skirt inflates to a tutu when she dances or pulls the ripcord on her waist.

characters.name: Wottingers
characters.description: The Wottingers are the supporting character who lives in a house with blue stripes near the Pontipines. Unlike their neighbours, the Wottingers appearances are usually rare. they were first organized in the episode pontipine children in the tombliboos trousers.they are also noticed at the dance at the Gazebo. Their living house is the blue doored house and they are seen waving to the pontipines. Wottingers Genders: males and females   First introduced: pontipine children in the toombliboos trousers  like: when they ride the ninky nonk   dislike: when there is no reply from the pontipines
